relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Moses calls the living generation to hear, learn, guard, and do.
Horeb is not sealed in the past; covenant addresses the present.
The living stand inside ancestral memory.
YHWH's voice meets the people face to face through fire.
Moses stands in the space between terror and teaching.
The Ten Words begin with liberation from slavery.
No rival power may stand before the Liberator's face.
The Divine cannot be reduced to a manufactured form.
Idolatry carries consequence across households and generations.
Covenant loyalty stretches farther than judgment.
The Name must not be carried into emptiness or manipulation.
Sabbath is guarded as holy time.
Work has a boundary.
Rest reaches children, workers, animals, and the sojourner alike.
The memory of slavery becomes someone else's right to rest.
Parents are honored within a life-giving social order, never through concealed abuse.
Life may not be murdered.
Covenant intimacy may not be betrayed.
Another's life and livelihood may not be taken.
Truth may not be weaponized against a neighbor.
Desire itself must be trained away from possession of another's household.
The great voice writes covenant into stone.
The leaders approach from darkness and fire.
They marvel that humanity can hear the living Divine and survive.
Awe becomes fear of annihilation.
The people know the encounter exceeds ordinary flesh.
They ask Moses to receive the teaching and promise to do it.
YHWH affirms the honesty of their request.
The Divine desire is for a heart that sustains reverence and well-being.
The people return to ordinary dwelling.
Moses remains to receive the fuller teaching.
The path asks for steadiness without domination.
Walking the commanded way seeks life, well-being, and endurance.

contemplative_note: The Decalogue begins not with demand but with liberation. Sabbath is its social center: no one in the household—including workers, immigrants, or animals—is allowed to become Egypt's slave again.
